pages.raw_text:
The Northern Pacific has taken away
from the lumber men of Spokane and
vicinity the through rate which was
granted to points in North Dakota on
the line of the Bismark, Washburn &
Great Falls road. No reason for this
has been given.


The Western Pine Shippers asso-
citation, through it's secretary, r. A.
Kellogg, took up the matter of this
rate about year ago and the through
rate to these points was granted last
July. The rate prior to that time
had been 40 cents to Bsmark from
Spokane, to which was added the local
rate to Washburn and other points
on the connecting lines. This made the
rate to these points about so cents
from Spokane.


Mr.. Kellogg took up the matter and
asked the Northern Pacific to grant
this through rate of 40 cents to these
points. The road finally granted his
request.


Word came today, however, that the
Accent rate had been abolished, TO
take effect May 81. 1t applies to pine
lumber, poles, shingles, etc. The sim
liar rate from the coast on fir lumber
